content.full_text:
June 14, 1976

Mr. George B. Scheer
55 Clarewood Lane
Oakland, California 94618

Dear Mr. Scheer:

This letter responds to your letter of May 28, 1976, in which you asked whether you should be registered as a lobbyist. Based on the facts stated in that letter, you are not a lobbyist and are not required to register. You presented the following information:

1. Spent about 5 hours in the last year and a half as an expert witness at hearings of the public utility Commission.

2. Spent as much as 40 hours a month attending hearings.

3. Spent about 5 hours total in last year and a half cross-examining witnesses at PUC hearings.

4. Spent as much as 10 days a month as follows:

   a. Writing exhibits to present;
   b. Writing questions for my attorney to use in cross examination;
   c. Assisting my attorney in writing briefs;
   d. Consulting my attorney in strategy.

5. Spent no money for any reason for personnel of PUC on or for lobbywork.

You stated that your lobbying efforts are concerned exclusively with the Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and that you had no contact with PUC personnel. In addition you spent no money to benefit PUC personnel or to support your lobbying efforts. The Commission has determined that an individual whose lobbying efforts are limited to influencing or attempting to influence an administrative agency such as the PUC which conducts public hearings, and provides public access to that record is not a lobbyist unless he spends 200 hours in any two consecutive months in such activities. (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239 (e) (2) (D)). The 200 hours includes time spent preparing written submissions and answers to inquiries if the material becomes part of the record.


It does not include time spent writing briefs, writing questions
for your attorney to use in cross examination, and consulting
your attorney on strategy. Even if all your other activities
are included, it does not appear that you spend 200 hours in
any two months on activities that are includable as "hours of
public testimony", as required by the Commission regulation.

Consequently, you are not a lobbyist within the meaning of
the Political Reform Act.

The time test set forth above would be substantially reduced
if you did make gifts to or have direct contact with PUC person-
nel. However this letter does not describe alternative time tests
because you have indicated that you do not make gifts to or have
contact with PUC personnel outside of the hearings.

I hope this information will be of assistance.

Sincerely,


Rich Eichman
Special Compliance Representative

RE:bw

bcc: Jeanne P. -


Question from
George B Lebherr
55 Clermont Lane Oakland 94618
registered as a lobbyist for Nurses State Leg
